originalClue: Andy's approach to law enforcement — he trusts this over force and rarely carries a loaded weapon.
originalAnswer: What is common sense (or reason, or talking it through)?

originalClue: Aunt Bee's specialty item she cooks for every special occasion, her pride and joy in the kitchen — though her pickles are another matter.
originalAnswer: What is her home cooking (or her pies)?

originalClue: In "The Pickle Story," Aunt Bee's homemade pickles are so bad that Andy and Barney privately call them by this unflattering nickname.
In "The Haunted House," the ghostly effects in the Rimshaw house — moving painting eyes and floating axe — were actually caused by these two people.
originalAnswer: Who are Otis Campbell and Big Jack Anderson (the moonshiner)?

originalClue: The song "Dooley," performed by the Darlings, tells the story of a man who makes this.
Otis's weekday job at the furniture factory — he dips items into a large vat of this substance.
In "Hot Rod Otis," Andy and Barney become concerned when Otis buys one of these — they fear he might use it recklessly.
The title of the first episode in which the Darling family appeared — Season 3, Episode 25.
The official title of the Andy Griffith Show theme song that Andy whistles and plays on guitar.
The full title of the Andy Griffith Show theme, including its subtitle distinguishing the vocal version from the instrumental.
originalAnswer: What is "The Fishin' Hole (Theme to The Andy Griffith Show)"?

originalClue: In "Divorce Mountain Style," the mountain custom that Charlene uses to divorce Dud — she performs a ceremony when she believes he looked at another woman.
originalAnswer: What is a mountain divorce ritual (symbolic ceremony)?
correctedAnswer: This is an odd question that sort of answers itself...see how its not really like trivia.

originalClue: In "The Loaded Goat," the reason Otis shows up at the jail only to find a goat in his usual cell.
originalAnswer: What is Andy and Barney locked the dynamite-eating goat there for safekeeping?
correctedAnswer: This is a long winded answer that is kinda being forced into the "jeopardy" format.

originalClue: Otis's full-time weekday occupation — he is a glue dipper at this type of business.
In "Hot Rod Otis," after the near-death scare trick, Otis vows to follow this rule about his new car.
originalAnswer: What is only drive it when sober (or never drive after celebrating)?
correctedAnswer: Actually...he sells the car, its too dangerous.

originalClue: The theme song Andy hums and plays on guitar in the opening credits — its official title.
notes: This question comes up all the time in the game.

originalClue: In "The Darling Baby," the name of Charlene and Dud's baby, born since the family's last visit to Mayberry.
originalAnswer: What is Andy? (named after Andy Taylor)
correctedAnswer: Pretty sure its Andilina or something that sounds like Andy, but it has a feminine sound.

originalClue: Barney's job title in the Mayberry Sheriff's Department.
Mayberry's sheriff, a widower who raises his son Opie and keeps his gun unloaded.
Ellie Walker worked at this drugstore on Main Street, owned by her uncle.
Andy's high school sweetheart who reappears at the class reunion in Season 3.
In this Season 4 episode, Andy and Barney discover that the Rimshaw house is haunted only by moonshiners hiding a still.
In this Season 2 episode, Andy and Barney organize their 20th high school reunion and Andy reconnects with his old flame Sharon DeSpain.
Opie's schoolteacher who becomes Andy's girlfriend, first appearing in "Andy Discovers America."
Andy's full name, including his middle name — the same as a famous president.
This is the name of Mayberry's county, in which the town is located.
originalAnswer: What is Mayberry County (or Macon County)?

originalClue: Gomer's one-word exclamation of amazement — a word borrowed from Captain Marvel comics.
In "Man in a Hurry," Barney lazily recites his entire Sunday itinerary to Malcolm Tucker, ending with this activity: "Then I go home and I ___ myself a little."
In "The Haunted House," Opie's baseball lands inside this old abandoned house on the edge of Mayberry.
Floyd Lawson's place of business, located next door to the courthouse.
In "The Pickle Story," Andy and Barney drove to this nearby town to buy replacement pickles for Aunt Bee's.
This is the very first episode of the series, in which Opie resists accepting the new housekeeper.
How Otis always gets into the Mayberry jail after a long night out — he has his own copy of this.
originalAnswer: What is his own key (to the jail)?

originalClue: In "Aunt Bee the Warden," Otis is sent to stay at the Taylor house because both jail cells are taken up by these four brothers.
In "Deputy Otis," Otis's brother's wife, who accompanies Ralph on the visit to Mayberry and also believes Otis is a deputy.
notes: This isn't really clear what the question is...

originalClue: The Darlings also perform the traditional song "Salty Dog" in their episode "The Darling Fortune." The poetic ballad "There Is a Time" was written by these two Dillards members.
originalAnswer: Who are Rodney Dillard and Mitch Jayne?

originalClue: The lyricist who wrote the words to "The Fishin' Hole," the vocal version of the Andy Griffith Show theme, in 1961.
